thesis: Development-stage mining company advancing the $1.3B Stibnite Gold Project in Idaho — one of the largest undeveloped gold deposits in the U.S. (6M+ oz gold, 148M lbs antimony). Broke ground in October 2025 after 9 years of permitting. America's answer to China's antimony export bans. $2B U.S. EXIM debt financing pending Board approval (Spring 2026). Full production targeted H2 2029. High-risk, high-reward with significant financing and execution risks but strong geopolitical tailwinds.

drivers: EXIM $2B financing closes at favorable terms (Spring 2026)
Gold price remains above $3,200/oz supporting project economics
Antimony prices spike on Chinese supply constraints and export bans
Construction stays on schedule with first production H2 2029
Additional strategic partnerships (defense, battery manufacturers) announced

risks: EXIM financing delayed or denied, forcing expensive alternative financing
Gold price drops below $2,600/oz impairing project economics
Major equity dilution (15-30%) required before production

base_case.assumptions: EXIM financing closes Q2 2026 with standard terms; gold price $3,000/oz; construction progresses with minor delays; first production H2 2029; equity dilution ~15%; antimony offtake agreements signed with defense contractors.

catalysts.event: U.S. EXIM Board consideration of $2B debt financing

key_risks: Financing risk: $2B EXIM debt not guaranteed; alternatives may be dilutive or expensive
Execution risk: First mine development with no operating history; cost overruns common
Commodity price risk: Gold and antimony price volatility affects project economics
Regulatory/environmental risk: Ongoing compliance and potential opposition in Idaho
Dilution risk: Multiple equity raises likely before 2029 production start
